Guardian Ad Litems Petition For Fees And Request For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John E. Rogers (by CourtCall) for guardian ad litem John E. Rogers, Jr.

The Court noted that it had received no objections to the petition for fees or appointment of successor trustee. He inquired about plans for filing a motion for change of venue. Mr. Rogers responded that the contemplated plan was to file twwo separate probate filings for the sub-trusts in Los Angeles where the children reside, with the sub-trusts to be administered there. The Court requested clarification if the current trust proceeding was to be administered in San Francisco or Los Angeles. Mr. Rogers said that the current trust was being administerd here in San Francisco, and that such would not be changed by the new sub-trusts. Following a brief recess, the Court determined that as the sub-trusts were created here pursuant to this court's prior order, he would grant the petition and set a status hearing re filing of bond in the amount of $59,000.00 for each sub-trustee for July 26, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204, with the status hearing to go off calendar if the required bonds were filed at least three days prior to the hearing. He set a further status hearing re change of venue to Los Angeles County for September 27, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204, with the status hearing to go off calendar if the petition for change of venue is filed at least three days prior to the hearing. The Court granted the petition (order signed).

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: John Boncato. (Not reported)

Petition To Approve Release And Settlement Agreement (Probate Code Sections 17200(B)(5) And (10))
Off Calendar Apr-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kenneth Philpot & James Holder for (trustee) Thomas Werdel. Patricia Bakst (via court call) for Patrick & Phillip Chandler. Bryan Hathorn (via court call) for (beneficiary) Bruce Chandler. Michael Overing (via court call) for Chandler Chandler. John Rogers (via court call) Guardian ad Litem.

Mr. Philpot stated that the examiner had concerns regarding the resignation and that it needs a formal amended petition, that they have submitted the settlement agreement, that they do not need to petition to appoint a successor trustee and that the procedure is to move without the necessity of a formal petition. The Court asked for responses to the objection of Ms. Chandler. Mr. Philpot stated that they are in his papers which are in front of the court and that she has no standing as the mother of the minor beneficiaries. Mr. Overling stated that the court needs to fully review the claim by Thomas Werdel and that they need to be sure that the Guardian ad Litem can properly represent the children. Mr. Rogers stated that he understands their concern, that he was in a car accident but experienced no mental damages, that he has reviewed the liability issues and that he is confident that he has analyzed and made representations for the children. Mr. Overling stated that he wants the timing regarding the sub-trusts as well as how they will be funded and when they will be available. Mr. Philpot responded stating as soon as the court approves the settlement agreement and addressed the court with his issues regarding the court's lack of procedure to confirm the receipt of courtesy copies. Mr. Rogers stated that his report asked for fees and that the court's notes say to file a fee petition and seek dismissal which he will do. The Court took the petition to approve release and settlement agreement under submission. The status on case and complaint file as petition in probate, the petition for instructions and to confirm right to payment of legal fees and the first amended petition for breach of fiduciary duty, etc., are off calendar.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR First Amended Petition For:1.Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; 2.Conspiracy To Breach Fiduciary Duty;3.Fraudulent Concealment; 4.Conspiracy To Defraud By Means Of Fraudulent Concealment;5.Negligence; 6.Declaratory Relief;7.Declaratory Relief ;Demand For Jury Trial
Off Calendar Oct-21-2015 Continued To Nov-30-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Respondent Thomas Werdels Demurrer To The First Amended Petition
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory Young for Philip & Patrick Chandler. Stuart Gordon for Thomas Werdel as an individual. Michael Brophy (via court call) for Bruce Chandler.

Mr. Young presented oral argument stating Mr. Werdel had a fiduciary relationship, that he had exclusive information the others did not have, that information was actively concealed by Mr. Werdel and Bruce Chandler, that facts were suppressed and urged that the matters be sustained with leave to amend. Mr. Gordon stated that the trustee is only responsible when acting as a trustee, that an individual cannot be held to fiduciary duty, that the petitioners can allege facts but it is a matter of law and that Mr. Werdel cannot face liability as an individual, only as a trustee. Mr. Brophy stated as to the second cause of action there is no way to amend, that as to fraudulent concealment the body of law points to certain types of relationships, that the only relationship here is father to adult children and that he had no obligation to disclose what is in the trust. The Court stated that it understands the argument and asked if they come back and plead Mr. Werdel in as a fiduciary will these get teed up again and why not simply give them a chance to amend capacity. Mr. Gordon stated that if petitioner's proceed against Mr. Werdel as trustee it is spelled out in probate law, that there is a question of law as to whether fiduciary duty exists, that they have a claim against him as a trustee, that there is no reason to drag him in as an individual and that Mr. Werdel is not a beneficiary but only acting as a trustee. Mr. Young stated that as to Mr. Werdel Mr. Gordon says he cannot conspire by himself, that they pleaded that he conspired with Bruce and that they never alleged that Mr. Werdel was conspiring by himself. The Court asked how you get to conspiracy as an individual. Mr. Young stated where does trustee stop and individual start and that the "whole" Mr. Werdel should be in the case. The Court asked why it would not be cleaner to sustain with leave to amend. Mr. Gordon stated that there is no justifiable reason to keep Mr. Werdel as an individual in this case, that an individual does not have a fiduciary duty and that there is no law to support keeping him in the case as an individual. The Court asked if there should be a carve out or if they should frame as Mr. Werdel only as a trustee. Mr. Brophy spoke of the basic law of conspiracy, that they must have committed an underlying tort, that the 2nd cause of action tries to hold a fiduciary liable for a non fiduciary act. Mr. Young stated that it is incorrect, that the 4 circumstances were stated in the papers, urged the court that if it is going to sustain to do so with leave to amend, that they can reframe the complaint, that the current petition is only the 1st amended, that as to the 7th cause of action they urge the court to sustain with leave to amend and that it is not anticipatory in nature. Mr. Gordon stated that Mr. Werdel is not entitled to fees as an individual, that everything is done as a trustee, that every cause of action refers to fiduciary duty and that Mr. Werdel has no fiduciary duty. Mr. Young cited 2 cases stating that they state the opposite. Mr. Brophy stated that everything being argued is against Mr. Werdel not Bruce Chandler and that a declaratory relief action is not correct. The Court requested Mr. Young to submit a proposed order in line with the courts tentative ruling and Mr. Gordon & Mr. Brophy to submit proposed orders without leave to amend no later than October 26, 2015 and Demurrer's will be under submission as of that date. The probate related matters currently on calendar for 11/2/15 are continued to November 30, 2015 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The discovery matters currently on calendar for 11/12/15 are continued to 12/9/15 at 2:00 p.m.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ken Philpot for Thomas Werdel. Gregory Young (via court call) for (beneficiaries) Patrick & Philip Chandler. Michael Brophy (via court call) for (beneficiary) Bruce Chandler. Melinda Tooch (via court call) for (respondent) Chandler Chandler. John Rogers, Guardian ad Litem.

Mr. Philpot reported that as of the end of last week they have agreed on a mediator, they are trying to schedule the mediation and that the parties have agreed to discuss interim stipulations. The Court asked if tuition payments have been made. Mr. Philpot stated that the grandchild scheduled to start college will not start until the winter semester, that they are working on the tuition for the middle school and high school and that it should be complete in one week. Ms. Tooch reported that the unrepresented beneficiary (Ashley Chandler) does not respond to counsel, has made no objections, that they need an agreement to proceed and suggests providing Ashley Chandler with documents via Federal Express and if there is no response within 7 days they can proceed. There were no objections to this suggestion. The Court asked Ms. Tooch to prepare a form of order to that effect and asked if there were any other issues regarding the extension of mediation. Mr. Philpot stated that it would be optimistic to think that it will be complete by October 1, 2015. Ms. Tooch stated that they had proposed a number of payments including summer school and were unable to get an agreement by Mr. Philpot. Mr. Rogers reported that the eldest minor recently turned 18, that he has verified the truncated tuition expenses, that he has contacted St. Brendans school where $5,000.00 in tuition is due, that the other school is a public school with voluntary tuition (suggested amount is $1,600.00)and feels that after reviewing the paperwork and meeting with the minors feels that the tuition is reasonable. The deadline to complete mediation is October 16, 2015. The status on case and status of complaint filed as petition in probate and the Petition for instructions, etc., and the First amended petition, etc., currently set for October 5, 2015 were continued to October 19, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
